As a result, an attacker can assign an arbitrary frontend user group to a newly registered or edited account, gaining unauthorized access to content and functionality restricted to privileged frontend user groups.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00352, "percentile": 0.26928}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-v348-vr4q-fv9p", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-29T22:58:05Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-v348-vr4q-fv9p", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-v348-vr4q-fv9p"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-46721"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-19T10:16:24Z", "published_at": "2026-05-19T12:31:39Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-46721", "https://typo3.org/security/advisory/typo3-ext-sa-2026-009", "https://github.com/FriendsOfPHP/security-advisories/blob/master/evoweb/sf-register/CVE-2026-46721.yaml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-v348-vr4q-fv9p"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/evoWeb/sf_register", "summary": "TYPO3 sf_register extension allows unauthorized assignment of frontend user groups", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-29T22:58:07Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-v348-vr4q-fv9p", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "14.0.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "evoweb/sf-register"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 14.0.0, < 14.0.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "13.2.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "evoweb/sf-register"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 13.2.4"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: TYPO3 sf_register extension allows unauthorized assignment of frontend user groups
